title: The AI boom didn't stop Google from hiring nearly 12,000 people
excerpt: Google's parent Alphabet said that it grew its head count by nearly 12,000 between June 2025 and June 2026.
content_html:
By Ana Altchek

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

and Madison Hoff

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

As companies make rounds of job cuts and double down on AI spend, Google's parent company Alphabet is expanding its workforce.

Alphabet reported blockbuster second-quarter earnings, with revenue climbing 24% year over year to $119.8 billion. The company revealed in its earnings report that it increased head count by 11,830 employees, from 187,103 to 198,933, between June 30, 2025 and June 30, 2026.

The chart below shows how Alphabet's workforce has grown from the end of the first quarter of 2025 through the end of the second quarter of 2026.

The biggest jump in Alphabet's head count of the last couple of years came in the second quarter of 2026, when the company added over 4,000 workers, accounting for more than one-third of net hiring over the past year.

Since 2022, companies including Google, Meta, Amazon, and Microsoft have cut thousands of jobs. Google laid off 12,000 employees in 2023 and has conducted several smaller rounds of cuts since, impacting thousands of employees in total.

Google employees from around the country rallied last week to demand stronger protections against layoffs. Roughly 4,500 employees signed a petition about job security addressed to CEO Sundar Pichai and three senior executives at the tech giant.

While Google didn't share which roles it has hired more of over the last year, the company said in its earnings on Thursday that it's prioritizing long-term AI growth and doubling down on its AI buildout.

The tech giant raised its 2026 capital expenditure outlook to between $195 billion and $205 billion, up from a previous estimate of up to $190 billion. The company said its demand for AI infrastructure continues to outpace available capacity.

Read next

Ana Altchek

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Madison Hoff

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Madison Hoff is a reporter on Business Insider’s economy team. She covers the labor market, inflation, spending, and other data. In addition to covering new estimates and trends, her workforce reporting includes career pivots, job searching, and side hustles.She also covers downsizing, particularly people selling their houses to pursue RV living. She has also reported on how much teachers spend out of pocket and what it’s like being a caregiver.Her stories often cover the state of the economy, what experts are saying, and how people are navigating the workplace or their careers.Previously, she was a junior reporter and data editorial fellow on the Strategy team.A few of her stories:

  • Job-market trend: Welcome to the 'Great Freeze': Why companies aren't firing, workers can't grow, and the unemployed can't get jobs
  • Job-market trend: Everyone's focused on AI — but it's aging Americans who are quietly rewiring the job market
  • Career pivot: I retired early from my federal job and took a part-time job at TJ Maxx. I'm happier and less stressed.
  • Downsizing/RV living: An empty-nester couple who traded in a $400K house for an $80K RV explain their favorite parts of retirement on the road
  • Job searching: People who haven't had steady work for at least a year are networking, doing temporary jobs, and soul-searching
  • Side hustles: A millennial who used side hustles to pay off debt explains the lucrative and easy ones she recommends
  • Teacher spending: A teacher who spent more than $5,000 of her own money to make a cozy classroom explains why it helps kids learn
  • Google
  • AI
  • Tech
  • More
  • Layoffs

sentences.en: Layoffs
sentences.zh: 裁员

content_html_zh:
作者：Ana Altchek 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。 和 Madison Hoff 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

随着各公司不断裁员并加大人工智能支出，谷歌母公司 Alphabet 正在扩大员工规模。

Alphabet 公布了惊人的第二季度财报，收入同比增长 24% 至 1198 亿美元。 该公司在财报中透露，2025年6月30日至2026年6月30日期间，员工人数增加了11,830人，从187,103人增至198,933人。

下图显示了从 2025 年第一季度末到 2026 年第二季度末 Alphabet 员工队伍的增长情况。

Alphabet 过去几年员工人数的最大增幅出现在 2026 年第二季度，当时该公司增加了 4,000 多名员工，占过去一年净招聘人数的三分之一以上。

自2022年以来，包括谷歌、Meta、亚马逊和微软在内的公司已经裁员数千人。 谷歌在 2023 年解雇了 12,000 名员工，此后又进行了几轮小规模的裁员，总共影响了数千名员工。

来自全国各地的谷歌员工上周举行集会，要求采取更强有力的保护措施，防止裁员。 大约 4,500 名员工签署了一份有关工作保障的请愿书，致函该科技巨头首席执行官桑达尔·皮查伊 (Sundar Pichai) 和三名高级管理人员。

虽然谷歌没有透露去年雇佣了更多的职位，但该公司在周四的财报中表示，它正在优先考虑人工智能的长期增长，并加倍加大人工智能的建设力度。

这家科技巨头将 2026 年资本支出预期上调至 1,950 亿美元至 2,050 亿美元，高于此前预计的 1,900 亿美元。 该公司表示，其对人工智能基础设施的需求继续超过可用容量。

阅读下一篇

Ana Altchek 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

Madison Hoff 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

麦迪逊霍夫是《商业内幕》经济团队的记者。 她报道了劳动力市场、通货膨胀、支出和其他数据。 除了涵盖新的估计和趋势之外，她的劳动力报告还包括职业转型、求职和副业。她还涵盖了缩小规模，特别是人们出售房屋以追求房车生活的情况。 她还报道了教师的自付费用以及作为一名看护者的感受。她的故事经常涵盖经济状况、专家的言论以及人们如何在工作场所或职业生涯中航行。此前，她是战略团队的初级记者和数据编辑研究员。她的一些故事：

  • 就业市场趋势：欢迎来到“大冻结”：为什么公司不裁员、工人无法成长、失业者找不到工作
  • 就业市场趋势：每个人都关注人工智能——但正在悄然重塑就业市场的是老龄化的美国人
  • 职业生涯支点：我提前从联邦工作岗位退休，并在 TJ Maxx 从事兼职工作。 我更快乐，压力更小。
  • 缩小规模/房车生活：一对空巢夫妇用价值 40 万美元的房子换了一辆价值 8 万美元的房车，讲述了他们在路上最喜欢的退休生活
  • 求职：至少一年没有稳定工作的人正在社交、做临时工作和自我反省
  • 副业：一位利用副业来偿还债务的千禧一代解释了她推荐的利润丰厚且简单的副业
